reviews.review_description: If I could old give -5 stars,.I would. This game has changed so much to the point where we're just encouraging lazy behavior. You don't want to work to be an imposter and hunt down people, no worries. Everyone can be an imposter. You can be a wrath. You can be a sheriff. You can be a vigilante. Everyone gets to be a killer and you can also start the game with four people and all of you except for one person is killing everyone before you even leave the cafeteria. This game is marketed for crybaby whiners who are still breastfeed and can't handle the word NO. This game used to be fun, now it's just full of a bunch of gen. Z's who aren't good at anything, but because you changed this game, all these bunch of b****** have found their home.

This game has a great concept, especially as a VR version of Among Us, but the execution has some serious issues.

First, the game can be buggy and the matchmaking system is horrible, why choose quick play for a game that’s nearly dead? It’d make sense to use the same system as normal among us. Another major problem is how updates have butchered the gameplay, the developers seem to just add bullcrap with no thought in mind. Several added roles, like the scanner or deputy, reduce the need for basic thinking, teamwork or agreement. For example, the scanner can instantly confirm whether someone is the impostor, and the deputy can force a vote without group agreement. These mechanics take away from the core strategy or detective skills that made the original game fun.

While hosts can adjust settings, most public lobbies still use these roles, which often leads to matches feeling less engaging, and more random and brain dead, games usually end in 3 minutes. Because of this, it can be difficult to find games that capture the original experience, which is what you’d think you’re paying for.

That said, the game can still be fun in the right lobby, but those are not very common, maybe 10% of the time. So if you’re looking for just original among us but in VR, keep your money. 😭

Absolutely amazing game, I’d honestly prefer it over the original Among Us. The huge factor that makes this game better than the original is the voice chat, that makes the game so much more engaging and social, not to mention the 3d factor, it makes the game more interactive and fun, instead of just walking around on a 2d map. However, there’s two huge issues with the game that keep it from being perfect. The first issue is not being able to get stardust for free. Yes, the game does give you a lot of cosmetics for free, but only hats, not gloves, skins, or anything else. The 2nd issue is the very poor matchmaking, if the game had a feature to search through lobbies instead of just quick play, that would make it much easier to find a lobby.

Everybody’s mean everybody bullies me no matter what I try to do everybody’s just mean I try to just ignore it, but everyone just gets meaner and meaner every single while we are going to and I tried to just fight back but then everyone’s kings up I mean, kicks me oh no matter what if I am imposter is the fight saying hey guys how do you kill? I’ll just say I’m impostor when I’m not and it’ll just kill me over and over.

reviews.review_title: THIS IS THE BEST GAME EVER!
reviews.review_description: After installing it, I was delighted. It's like the good old Among Us, but the tasks are more interesting, the animations are cooler, and everything is much better than the regular 2D Among Us! I'm very happy with the purchase. Keep going! You can do it! ❤️

A few days ago, I decided to come back to Among Us VR because I was starting to get bored while playing VR and I don’t know if the game is getting better or worse.

For starters, it seems like loading into the game or a game has gotten a bit longer and there has been some connection problems with the game.
There is also a lot of glitches I have found like getting stuck in the cosmetic room, the game breaking because their are 0 imposters, or just people just walking through locked door (not in ghost form). 

The people aren’t mostly better either. One hand, you got kids being kids and just voting out random people for bad/no reasons, and on the other hand you just get straight up racism. I have seen many games where half the lobby votes out the person because they are wearing black or brown.

It also doesn’t help that there are signs showing up that the Devs don’t even care for Among Us VR anymore. Like today, a new map came out to the public and it’s just a copy and paste of the second map, just with a new look and moved around rooms. The worse part is the ejection sense, just an empty void with a lava pond. It’s just straight up lazy work.

I was originally going to point out why the game still has some good to it, like there still being nice players to have a fun time with, or the event gamemodes (which I think should still stay even after the event is over). However, when reading back on what I had, I think it’s best to agree that the game has fallen down.

Update:

Putting this here because it is really bad. Recently, I encountered this glitch where the game freezes, rotates you around, and just turns off your entire headset. Couldn’t turn it back on for the past 10 minutes before it booted itself back on, I thought Among Us VR broke my headset. Didn’t think much on it though until earlier when it tried it again, however this time I left the game before it could continue.

This is basically a headset breaker in the code Among Us VR. Even though my headset is fine after 10 minutes of being off, if someone’s headset is in some kind of bad shape and they came across this glitch, there done for.

Update1:

Among Us VR latest event is just a cry that the Devs don’t care about VR anymore. If you’re wondering why I say this, here is a brief description of it.

Basically in this event, there is a role called Hero who is able to kill the Imposter.

That’s it, just a new limited time role, no abilities, no powers, just a crewmate who can just kill random people, even if they’re not Imposters.
Doesn’t help that the phrase “With great power come great responsibility” doesn’t go well with Among Us VR kids. Literally in the first game of the event I was in, the “Hero” said he’s just going to ghost everybody.

Something else I want to point out is the updated terms and services. Now I’m not saying it bad they updated it. However, knowing part of the players are either kids being bad or are just racist people. I would say it’s the best way to lose a lot of the player base.

Also for those who are wondering about the terms and services update. It’s just the game recording audio and putting it in a personal data base.

Update2:

Remembered I made this so thought I’d update this since it’s kicking up again.

When I first started playing again, I was having a blast. Met cool people, loved the new added cosmetics, and was joyful to see Tag and Roles make a comeback permanently.

While I am happy to see the game evolving for the better, I’ve noticed that the complaints I made about players still stands￼. Not just with children or racists, but also with hackers being thrown in the mix now.

One of the games I was playing ￼had a hacker who not only killed the imposter but also repeatedly took photos on the camera that caused the whole lobby to break. I had to stop playing skied (or however you spell it) because that’s where they’re all at.

It disappoints me how I don’t see any moderation for 3D￼ as it reminds me that we’ll be stuck with players who just do whatever they want with nothing that can be done about it. Which ruins the motivation on playing this game again.

Anyways I’m gonna probably stop updating this review as I don’t see anything changing other than new maps, roles, and gamemodes.

Goodnight everyone

Update3:

This is probably for both games but The Sheriff role made me quit the game again. While on paper it sounds fun and useful, it ruins the whole purpose of the game.￼ Like isn’t Among Us supposed to be about talking to one another about who did it instead of having one person decide??

That’s not even the reason why I came back and updated this. It’s the fact that Imposters get this role too??

Just now me and this other person were doing so well getting kills without notice. That was until we got a meeting and I got Sheriff. Their was a lot of suspicion that it was the other person, so I got stuck￼ killing them and getting ejected later, killing someone else ￼and ruining both our chances, or doing nothing and still ruining our chances. Either way, I lose.

Wouldn’t even be a big deal either￼ if Imposter wasn’t the hardest role to get, even with 3 Imposters enabled. I don’t even remember the last time I was Imposter without immediately winning/getting voted out.￼￼

I hate how this is becoming more of a journal than a review now, but who knows. I might be back a year or two later updating this review again.

At least the show was a 5 stars though.￼

reviews.review_title: Good game–A lot more to be desired.
reviews.review_description:
As someone who started playing the OG 2D Among Us back in 2021 or so (back before 15 people lobbies were a thing) and also who recently bought a Quest 3S, playing Among Us 3D feels like a refreshing experience for the general whodunit gameplay loop. However, there are going to be some multiple things I'm going to nitpick and wish that were different (and existent)

1: Lack of maps. Yeah, this isn't really anything new, but I find it kind of sad that there are only 3 maps in Among Us 3D, whereas the OG had double that. Yeah, the Mess Hall is a rather original addition, but that doesn't change the fact that it leaves the experience very... empty. Not that I can really complain too much about the maps, they're all stunning and appealing to play on, and the tasks feel more fun to play here than the og Among Us.

2: Roles(..?) We have a few reprised roles here, the Engineer and the Tracker. We also have a few other roles, such as Deputy, Wraith, Scanner, and Vigilante. All of these roles are good in their own sense and mind, but there's just a few major issues with them. Firstly, the amount of roles you can set in a lobby that you host are VERY limited (including how you want cooldowns). Seems like a minor nitpick, but the OG Among Us had it so you can have the maximum player count (15) as the amount of players for said role. So the difference there is shockingly night and day. While we're on the topic, let's talk about the Deputy, Scanner, and Vigilante. Where do I even start. While I use the Scanner role in my lobby, I have it so they're extremely limited. Why? Scanner has the ability to reveal who the impostor is in just the single click of a button if they guess right, and BOOM, the game's over. Same thing with the Vigilante, they have the ability to kill AS A CREWMATE. While on paper that sounds like a good game concept, the execution shown for the role is VERY poor, given how the game can just abruptly end out of nowhere (and plus you might end up with trolls. These two factors are why I have Vigilante turned off). Lastly, the Deputy. Again, on paper, the idea of a random player being selected to choose anyone to be voted off in the time limit (and if they don't catch them, they themselves get voted off) CAN be interesting, but like the Vigilante, the execution is just disappointing. Yes you can set it so where it's occasional that the Deputy will appear, but that doesn't negate the fact how bleak and boring Deputy is in any round. 

TL;DR for the Roles nitpick, Deputy, Scanner, and Vigilante are boring roles that can instantly end a game. I recommend having Scanner limited to 1 scan to make it more crucial. (Even then, when it comes to a situation where it's you, another crewmate that you scanned negative, and the impostor, it's immediately easy to tell who it is if the impostor left the person you scanned alive)

3: Cosmetics (or lack thereof) All of the cosmetics seem to be locked behind a pay wall with no free currency (like beans for example from OG Among Us) to earn new accessories for yourself. When you purchase Among Us 3D, you have a small variety of hats to choose from, but that's about it. All the skins and gloves are locked behind the Stardust pay wall. Pets also don't seem to be a thing either, which is unfortunate.

Overall, Among Us 3D is a fun experience. I personally have no regrets about buying it. However, I SEVERELY recommend hosting your own public lobbies instead of joining lobbies via quick play, as quick play lobbies will often include the roles that make the game boring (or just flat out Insufferable to play in general). It's definitely a lot more fun playing Among Us with Voice Chat in it, and this is something that I most definitely wish the OG Among Us had.

reviews.review_title: Love the game, but it lacks just a bit
reviews.review_description:
I love this game, it has many fun moments with all the people I meet, obviously some people here and there are “unique” but nonetheless overall a great experience. My only concern/question is that will the game get closer to the state of the main game? Like, will we eventually get cosmicubes and the roles of the main game, as well as the maps like Mira hq, cause I’m sure all for it, I just feel like this isn’t as up to date as it could be. 
 
    And also don’t get me wrong, I love the events this game does it makes it really great but at the same time, roles like deputy should be their own mode like how tag is. And again about how “up to date” this game could be, I hope you guys (Innersloth)  could add like a game filter/menu so we can see what maps are available and what settings they have like the main game has.

I really enjoy this game, but there is one thing I don’t like.

When someone presses the emergency button, there is no voting. Instead, one random player is chosen and given a lasso. Only that player can decide who gets thrown into space.

I don’t understand why this works this way because it feels unfair. A random player gets all the power, and everyone else has no say in the decision.

Other than that, I really like the game.

Among Us 3D is really fun! When i’m bored I just hop on and play Among Us BUT 3D. In my opinion this edition is better than the 2D Among Us. It has several different roles and it has real proximity voice chat which is fun. Even though the game can be toxic and not fun at some points, you just gotta find the perfect lobby.

I would 100% recommend this game if you’re a teenager, but if you’re looking to get this as under 12 years old, I wouldn’t recommend it to you because of the toxic community.

Overall, you should get it! It’s very engaging and fun and better than the 2D Among Us we know and love.

We all know the original among us. Let me tell you how 3d version works. Here you have voice chat. You can play with vr and PC players. There's 2 game modes. 1st the original. Here you get in a lobby with a limit of 10 people total. There are settings only avaliable by the host (1st to join) There's also many different roles. We have vigilante (can kill but still a crewmate) engineer (can vent as a crewmate) scanner (can see if a person if a imposter or a crewmate). Tracker (can temporarily see the location of somone for a limited time. This desperately needs a buff). And there's wraith (only alt imposter role. You can kill yourself and kill people while dead. While this can be turned off, you also have music that everyone living person can hear and he can still sabotage) then we have the originals. Crewmate (no special abilities) and imposter (can kill. The guy you need to vote out. The guy who can sabotage). And then special roles. 1st guardian angel (1st few people who die can respawn as a guardian angel instead of a ghost. Its the same as ghost in every way shape and form but it can put a temporary shield over living people to protect them from one hit from imposter. They are also faster than everyone) then who i hate the most, deputy (incredibly overrated character. Instead of voting one person (imposter or crewmate) can be a deputy and manually has to hit someone to vote them out. If they vote no one then they die instead). Note once you die you spawn as a ghost. No one can hear you in this mode. You also cannot phase through walls. Crewmatws win by either voting everyone out or completing all their tasks. Imposter win by killing everyone. Or sabotaging crucial place and no one fixes it in time.

2nd game modes. Tag. Here there's 2 roles. 1st is infected. You can kill people and they will spawn as infected. Its obvious who is infected. They are also slightly slower than normal. Next is crewmate. Your average guy. This time everything you complete a task you get a ability. These are great without being too OP. Crewmates win by completing all their tasks and infected win by infecting everyone. 

There's no real reward for winning. The in game currency is pay to win only. But all it can buy are cosmetics. You already have a good handful of free cosmetics when you join in so no worries there. There is VC. I do have to say that (just like every other vc game) you will encounter bullies. There is a very reliable vote kick system. ALSO NOTE. YOU CAN DOSABLE VOICECHAT SERVERS AND YOU CAN RESORT TO A LIST OF PRESET MESSAGES. Over all its a great game. I have no promblems. Its everything I asked for in a 3d among us game.

I played the phone version of among us almost since it came out and I loved the BEGINNING of it, where there were a few roles and it was an ACTUAL STRATEGY GAME. Now it is nearly impossible to win as imposter because of all these new roles. The one time I get vigilante, the scanners just scan and vote both imposters out on the spot. And another thing, what happened to beans as payment? It was so simple and easy to get while you still could buy them. Now if you want any actually GOOD cosmetics, they are stuck behind a paywall. Usually I’m good when things change, but now you have gone too far.

Honestly I didn't expect to get attached to this game but after playing some rounds I find it kinda funny with the voice chat feature but I wish it was on mobile

The game itself is really fun, but the community gets me questioning the Moderation. For example, there was a person in game that said he gets reported ten to nine times a day and does not receive any warnings or bans. Please, fix this problem.
I LOVE IT
Really fun, it is the only constant fun game I go back to.
